title: 3D Printed Face Shield with Closed Top
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a65cm","text":"This protocol is for building face shields with closed tops using the print template created by Hanoch Hemmerich on Thingiverse.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"d3jlp","text":"Act as if you were infected by the COVID-19 virus. Wear a face mask and a fresh pair of gloves when collecting each batch of printed parts. Store the parts immediately in a sealable bag.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":51}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6qq90","text":"Talk with whoever you’re making the shields for, let them know about your manufacturing environment","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ebfml","text":"There is still debate about how long the virus survives on plastic, but most sources mention 2-3 days. That means that by letting the packed face shields sit for 2-3 days before distributing them, you’ll greatly reduce risk of transmission","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6emsh","text":"Do not store the entire stock in one place, minimize the risk of cross contamination","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":42}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"8dh2p","text":"Disclaimer: Building a face shield as represented in this protocol could potentially help increase protection however there is no guarantee of such an outcome.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"7mm2g","text":"3D printer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f2rhu","text":"filament spool","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9vc57","text":"overhead transparency or laminated laminating foil (DIN A4 size)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ag74o","text":"strap ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Print the head part of the face shield with the 3D printer. Visera_Fija_Cerrada_v0.4_con_autolock.stl
Place the overhead transparency or laminated laminating foil on the head part.
Put a strap through both ends of the head part of the shield to fix it on your head.
creator: Julia Rossmanith
